Name: Halberdiers
Flavor Text: The backbone of every battle the Empire has survived.

Name: Crossbowmen
Flavor Text: Aim at the gap in the armour. There is always a gap.

Name: Handgunners
Original Text: Armour piercing: enemy armour one worse. Up to 1 per 1000pts can replace Crossbowmen and count toward their min/max.
Current Text: Armour piercing: enemy armour one worse. Up to 1 per 1000pts can replace Crossbowmen and count toward their min/max.
Flavor Text: The roar of powder ends more arguments than reason.

Name: Knights
Flavor Text: Steel-clad, debt-free, and pointed at the enemy.

Name: Weird Enchantment
Original Text: Cast on any enemy unit (no LoS needed). Lasts until end of opposing player's next turn. The unit moves at half pace in all situations, even when charging. The unit counts all enemies as terrifying (-1 Attack), even if normally immune to terror. If the unit would normally cause terror it ceases to do so. Undead and Daemon targets do not count enemies as terrifying but all other penalties apply.
Current Text: Cast on any enemy unit (no LoS). Lasts until end of opponent's next turn. Unit moves at half pace in all situations, even when charging. The unit counts all enemies as terrifying (-1 Attack), even if normally immune to terror. If the unit would normally cause terror it ceases to do so. Undead and Daemon targets do not count enemies as terrifying but all other penalties apply.

Name: Helblaster
Original Text: Range 30cm. Attacks vary by range: 01-10cm = 8 attacks, 11-20cm = 4 attacks, 21-30cm = 2 attacks. Targets count Armour one worse than normal. If any 1s are rolled when shooting, count them: 1-2 = Fizzle (resolve attacks normally); 3 = Misfire (no shots this turn); 4+ = Ka-boom! (Helblaster destroyed, no hits struck on enemy unless shooting at a charging foe, in which case 6 hits are automatically inflicted — make armour saves as normal for Helblaster hits).
Current Text: Range 30cm. Atk by range: 0–10cm = 8, 11–20cm = 4, 21–30cm = 2. Target must be visible; shoots in a straight line, never into combat. Each time it fires roll for malfunction: on a 1, roll again — 1–3 = destroyed, 4–6 = no fire this turn (fires normally next turn). Cannot be driven back by shooting; crew hold their ground on that result.

Name: Steam Tank
Original Text: Always counts as defended (5 or 6 to hit). Cannot be driven back or routed by shooting. Cannot brigade. No character may join it. 360 degree vision. Shoots to front, side or rear against closest enemy target (30cm range, counts armour one worse). On a double-6 order, roll Malfunction: 1=Destroyed; 2=Broken Down (cannot move ever again); 3=Stuck (no move/shoot this turn); 4=Commander Slain (-1 Command permanently); 5=Momentary Halt; 6=Steam Overload (cannot move but shoots with 6 Attacks).
Current Text: Always def (5+ to hit). Cannot be driven back or routed by shooting. No brigade; no character may join. 360° vision. Shoots 30cm, armour piercing. On double-6 order: roll Malfunction chart. In combat: may advance, cannot pursue. At badly hurt: halved to 3H/2A.

Name: Skeleton Cavalry
Flavor Text: They rode in life. In death, they ride still.

Name: Raise Dead
Original Text: Cast on a combat engagement within 30cm (no LoS needed). Creates a 3-stand Skeleton unit placed in contact with the engagement or supporting a friendly unit. If stands cannot be legally placed the spell fails. Raised dead do not count as charging and are ignored for breakpoint and victory points. Only one Raise Dead per engagement per turn. Placement cannot split the engagement.
Current Text: Cast on a combat within 30cm (no LoS). Places a 3-stand Skeleton unit in contact with the engagement; fails if stands cannot be legally placed. Raised dead do not count as charging; ignored for breakpoint and VPs. One Raise Dead per engagement per turn. Placement cannot split the engagement.

Name: Chaos Warriors
Flavor Text: Forged in the wastes. Bound to slaughter.

Name: Marauder Horsemen
Flavor Text: They ride south to take what the gods demand.

Name: Chaos Hounds
Flavor Text: They have tasted blood from a thousand battlefields.

Name: Rage of Chaos
Original Text: Cast on a friendly unit engaged in combat and within range (no LoS needed). Lasts for the following Combat phase. The unit gains bonus Rage attacks — roll D6 repeatedly (up to 1 per stand), adding results. Stop at any time, but if a repeat value is rolled all attacks hit the Chaos unit instead. Non-rebounded bonus attacks apply each round of combat. Rebounded attacks are struck only once in the first round, not each round.
Current Text: Cast on a friendly unit in combat within range (no LoS needed). Lasts for the next Combat phase. Each stand gains +1 attack. The unit causes terror (-1 atk penalty to enemy in combat if not already suffering it). Only one unit per combat engagement may benefit from Rage at a time.

Name: Orc Warriors
Flavor Text: They live to fight. Everything else is waiting.

Name: Goblins
Flavor Text: Countless as cockroaches and nearly as brave.

Name: Black Orcs
Flavor Text: Even other Orcs give them room to swing.

Name: Boar Riders
Flavor Text: The boar doesn't care what it tramples. Neither does the rider.

Name: Rock Lobber
Flavor Text: Sometimes the rock comes back. That's also fine.

Name: Spearmen
Flavor Text: Their phalanx has held since before men had kingdoms.

Name: Archers
Original Text: +1 to shooting dice rolls (hit on 3+ in open, 4+ defended, 5+ fortified).
Current Text: +1 to shooting dice rolls (hit on 3+ in open, 4+ defended, 5+ fortified).
Flavor Text: Each arrow is loosed with the patience of centuries.

Name: Silver Helms
Flavor Text: They charge as silver light cuts through winter fog.

Name: Reavers
Flavor Text: Swift enough to be gone before the enemy turns around.

Name: Dragons
Original Text: Fly (move 100cm). Dragon Rider units and units with a Dragon-riding character cause terror. Dragon breathes fire: 20cm range, 3 Attacks at 4+ (rider must have joined a unit, not engaged in combat). Generals, Wizards and Heroes can ride Dragons (+3 Attacks). Dragon Riders with 4-5 hits become Badly Hurt — Hits and Attacks halved (3 Hits, 3/2 Attacks). A Dragon ridden by a lone character (not in a unit) cannot breathe fire.
Current Text: Fly (100cm). Dragon Rider units and units with a Dragon-riding character cause terror. Breath attack: 20cm, 3 atk, hits on 4+; used in addition to normal attacks. Magic items cannot augment breath. At 4–5 hits (not engaged): badly hurt, halved to 3H/3A/2 breath attacks. Individual Dragon mounts: rider remounts on the surviving stand.

Name: Warriors
Original Text: Tough core infantry. One Handgunner unit per 1000pts may replace a Warrior unit and count for Warrior min/max.
Current Text: Tough core infantry. One Handgunner unit per 1000pts may replace a Warrior unit and count for Warrior min/max.
Flavor Text: Short of stature. Long of grudge. Hard of skull.

Name: Cannon
Flavor Text: Dwarf engineering: loud, reliable, and deeply satisfying.

Name: Rangers
Original Text: One unit of Rangers per 1000pts may infiltrate instead of deploying normally. Issue an infiltration order from any character (ignoring dense terrain penalties and Command range limits) to a point in dense terrain or on any table edge except the enemy's. On success the unit appears there, using that as their first order. Rangers can pursue any type of retreating enemy despite being infantry.
Current Text: One unit per 1000pts may infiltrate instead of deploying normally. Give an infiltration order to a point in dense terrain or on any table edge except the enemy’s — Cmd range is unlimited for this order. On success, the unit appears there. Cannot appear within 20cm of any enemy unit at deployment.

Name: Clanrats
Original Text: Strength in Numbers: brigades unlimited size.
Current Text: Strength in Numbers: brigades unlimited size.
Flavor Text: Many blades make light work of courage.

Name: Stormvermin
Flavor Text: Stronger, meaner, and marginally less likely to flee.

Name: Rat Ogres
Flavor Text: Madness given sinew. Hunger given fists.

Name: Screaming Bell
Original Text: Cannot move using initiative. Can only move in a brigade with infantry. Cannot be driven back or routed by shooting. Destroyed if forced to retreat from combat. Will pursue, advance and fall back only while touching a Skaven infantry unit at start and end of move. All friendly units touching the Bell are immune to terror. Heroes/Warlocks within 30cm get +1 Command; enemy characters within 30cm get -1 Command.
Current Text: Cannot move on initiative. May only move in a brigade with infantry. Destroyed if forced to retreat from combat. Cannot be driven back. Each Shooting phase, roll D6 per enemy unit within 20cm: on 6, that unit is confused (no save). Gains the best armour save of any friendly infantry within 10cm.

Name: Saurus
Flavor Text: Cold eyes. Colder blood. Older than memory.

Name: Salamanders
Original Text: Salamander stands are not deployed independently — any Skink infantry unit may add one Salamander stand (making 4 stands total). Salamanders share unit Armour, fight as part of the unit, and casualties never count for Command penalties. Salamander stands never cause irregular formation no matter how placed. The whole unit (including Salamander) gains Salamander Venom: enemy Armour saves suffer -1 penalty (e.g. 5+ requires a 6).
Current Text: Not deployed independently. Any Skink infantry unit may add one Salamander stand (+pts cost). Salamanders fight with their Skink unit. In shooting, use the Salamander’s own attacks (not the Skinks’). In combat, resolve Salamander attacks separately using its own Attack value. Destroyed if its Skink unit is destroyed. Max one Salamander per Skink unit.

Name: Slann Mage Palanquin
Original Text: The Slann General has no personal Command value (Command 0) but The Slann cannot give orders himself. Instead Skink characters within 20cm use Divine Guidance (deduct -1 from order dice). If DG fails, Divine Guidance cannot be used by any other characters for the rest of that turn, and those characters get +1 to their first order rolls. The Slann casts spells as a Wizard and can re-roll failed spells (except on natural 1). Can cast through Skink Shamans within 20cm.
Current Text: The Slann General has Cmd 0 and cannot give orders — only Skink Heroes command. The Slann casts spells with +1 to casting rolls; all Wizards within 20cm also get +1. May ride a Stegadon (terror, breath attack retained). When on Stegadon, the Slann’s full-table Cmd range applies to his unit only. Up to 2 Palanquin Bodyguards (Temple Guard) may be attached.

Name: Men-at-Arms
Flavor Text: They serve so their lords need not bleed.

Name: Bowmen
Flavor Text: Every peasant owes his lord a thousand arrows.

Name: Squires
Flavor Text: Eager to prove themselves. Less eager to die.

Name: Trebuchet
Original Text: Uses a 40x60mm base. Can move but cannot shoot in the same turn it moves. Destroyed if driven back more than 10cm by shooting or if forced to retreat from combat. Range 80cm. Fortified counts as defended, defended counts as open. No armour rolls allowed. Cannot shoot at charging enemies. Can shoot over the heads of friendly troops on lower ground. Not affected by Gerroff!!! spell.
Current Text: 40×60mm base. Can move or shoot, not both. Destroyed if driven back 10cm+ by shooting. Requires LoS; shoots straight from barrel, 60cm range. Uses Stone Thrower rules (hits def and fort units). Malfunction on 1: roll again — 1–3 = destroyed, 4–6 = no fire this turn. Cannot shoot into combat.

Name: Axemen
Flavor Text: Kislev winters breed hard men with harder axes.

Name: War Wagon
Original Text: Cannot charge and moves 20cm at full pace. Moving up to 5cm can end in laager formation (360 degree fire, 4+ Armour). Moving more than 5cm must end in column formation. Either stand can be removed as casualty; can only move while the team stand remains. 30cm range, counts enemy Armour one worse. Can shoot over lower friendly troops. In column: cannot pursue but retreats normally (not destroyed). In laager: will neither pursue nor retreat (not destroyed if forced to retreat), continues to count as defended, enemy can continue to fight but with no pursuit bonuses, cannot make way. Infantry touching laagered wagon's short edge counts as defended (first combat round and for shooting).
Current Text: Cannot charge; moves 20cm. May end a move of up to 5cm in laager (360° vision). Laager: 3 shots/30cm, 6 attacks from any direction; cannot move next turn. Line: 2 shots/30cm, moves 20cm, def. No brigade with cavalry. Never driven back — retreats 5cm in formation instead. In combat: no advance or pursue. If routed in laager: destroyed. If forced onto a unit: both destroyed.

Name: Crossbowmen
Original Text: Repeating crossbow: shoot twice at ≤15cm (6 attacks), once at ≤30cm (3 attacks). 1 attack/stand when shooting at chargers.
Current Text: Repeating crossbow: shoot twice at ≤15cm (6 attacks), once at ≤30cm (3 attacks). 1 attack/stand when shooting at chargers.
Flavor Text: Bolts tipped in spite, aimed with cold precision.

Name: Dark Riders
Original Text: Repeating crossbow: shoot twice at ≤15cm. 1 attack/stand at chargers.
Current Text: Repeating crossbow: shoot twice at ≤15cm. 1 attack/stand at chargers.
Flavor Text: Fleet as malice, cruel as winter on the open sea.

Name: Zombies
Flavor Text: Death is not the end. It is merely a change of orders.

Name: Grave Guard
Flavor Text: They remember what it was to be feared.

Name: Raise Dead
Original Text: Cast on a combat engagement within 30cm (no LoS needed). Creates a 3-stand Skeleton unit placed in contact with the engagement. If stands cannot be legally placed the spell fails. Raised dead do not count as charging and are ignored for breakpoint and victory points. Only one Raise Dead per engagement per turn. Placement cannot split the engagement.
Current Text: Cast on a combat within 30cm (no LoS). Places a 3-stand Skeleton unit in contact with the engagement; fails if stands cannot be legally placed. Raised dead do not count as charging; ignored for breakpoint and VPs. One Raise Dead per engagement per turn. Placement cannot split the engagement.

Name: Spearmen
Flavor Text: They guard the gate as their fathers did before them.

Name: Bowmen
Flavor Text: The desert wind carries arrows as readily as sand.

Name: Knights
Flavor Text: Silk robes beneath steel plate, death beneath both.

Name: Magic Carpets
Flavor Text: The sky is just another battlefield.

Name: Crossbowmen
Flavor Text: They sell their aim to whoever meets the asking price.

Name: Handgunners
Flavor Text: Loud, acrid, and devastatingly convincing.

Name: Swordsmen
Flavor Text: A clean blade keeps better than loyalty.

Name: Light Cavalry
Flavor Text: In, out, and gone before the dust settles.

Name: Knights
Flavor Text: Their lance is for hire. Their honour, negotiable.

Name: Pikemen
Original Text: Never benefit from defended or fortified status in dense terrain or on fortress walls. Count as defended against cavalry or chariots charging their front (first combat round only). Based as cavalry but move as infantry. Can only give/receive support from infantry or other Pikemen stands facing the same direction, touching flank with whole flank. No support from behind or in front.
Current Text: No def or fort benefit in dense terrain or on fortress walls. Count as def in the open vs. frontal attacks (enemy hit on 5+). Flanked or rear attacks: no defensive benefit, normal hits apply. Cannot charge on initiative. +1 atk when charged to the front by cavalry or chariots; no +1 for charging.

Name: Ironguts
Flavor Text: The gut-plate is decorative. The fists are not.

Name: Sabretusks
Flavor Text: They outrun the wind and outfight the foolish.

Name: Scrap Launcher
Flavor Text: Waste nothing. If it fits in the bucket, it flies.

Name: Warriors
Flavor Text: Painted in woad, hardened by rain, unbowed by empire.

Name: Slingers
Flavor Text: A stone well-thrown needs no blessing to kill.

Name: Wolfhounds
Flavor Text: The wolves of Albion do not bark before they bite.

Name: Fenbeast
Original Text: Cannot be deployed normally. A Druid may summon Fenbeasts using the Summon Fenbeast spell. Fenbeasts can only be ordered by Druids and cannot brigade with any other unit type. Fenbeasts never use initiative to move. They can move through bogs as open terrain. The army cannot have more Fenbeasts on the table at any time than it has Druids. Fenbeasts are not counted towards break point or victory points.
Current Text: Cannot be deployed normally; summoned by a Druid via the Summon Fenbeast spell. Causes terror. If their Druid is slain, Fenbeasts are confused until a new Druid moves within 20cm. Regeneration: at the start of each Combat phase, restore one destroyed stand in base contact. Badly Hurt: at 3 hits, attacks halved to 2.

Name: Spear Chukka
Flavor Text: It don't need to be accurate. Just frequent.

Name: Halberdiers
Flavor Text: Their faith is in steel, not scripture.

Name: Knights
Flavor Text: Their crusade has no end. Their enemies have no mercy.

Name: Divine Curse
Original Text: Cast on any enemy unit within range (no LoS needed). Lasts until end of opposing player's following turn. The unit moves at half pace in all situations, even when charging. The unit counts all enemies as terrifying (-1 Attack penalty) even if normally immune to terror. If the unit would normally cause terror it ceases to do so. Undead and Daemon targets do not count enemies as terrifying but all other penalties apply.
Current Text: Cast on any enemy unit within range (no LoS). Lasts until end of opponent's next turn. Unit moves at half pace, even when charging. Unit counts all enemies as terrifying (-1 Atk) even if normally immune. If unit normally causes terror, it ceases to. Undead/Daemon targets do not count enemies as terrifying but all other penalties apply.

Name: Flagellants
Original Text: Always charge on initiative; cannot be given orders instead. Never evade. Cannot be driven back by shooting and do not roll for drive backs. Must pursue or advance if victorious. Immune to terror — no -1 Attack modifier.
Current Text: Always charge on initiative; cannot be ordered instead. Never evade. Cannot be driven back by shooting; no drive-back rolls. Must pursue or advance if victorious. Unaffected by terror (no -1 atk). +1 atk vs. Monsters (giants, dragons, etc.). If any stands survive a combat round, the enemy scores full VPs. If the unit is destroyed completely, neither side scores VPs.

Name: Black Orcs
Flavor Text: Chained in the forge-pits. Unchained on the field.

Name: Bull Centaurs
Flavor Text: Half craftsman, half beast — wholly murderous.

Name: Glade Riders
Flavor Text: They move through the forest like wind through leaves.

Name: Bestigors
Flavor Text: They are what the forest becomes when left to its rage.

Name: Centigors
Flavor Text: Wild, drunk, and twice as fast as reason permits.

Name: Chaos Hounds
Flavor Text: Born hungry. Never sated. Always faster than you.

Name: Gor and Ungor Ambushers
Original Text: Up to half the Gor units (rounded down) and half the Ungor units (rounded down) may be designated Ambushers. Ambushers are deployed during any Command phase rather than at the start. Issue an Ambush order to a point in dense terrain or on any table edge except the enemy's (ignoring Command range limits but applying distance and enemy proximity penalties). On success, place one stand at the point and deploy the rest in formation around it. The Ambush order counts as their first order. Ungor have 15cm shooting range.
Current Text: Up to half Gor units and half Ungor units (both rounded down) may be Ambushers. They are not deployed at the start. Each Cmd phase, instead of ordering them, place one in dense terrain not occupied by enemy and at least 20cm from any enemy — this counts as their move. Each unit may ambush once. If the terrain is within 20cm of an enemy at game start, no ambush there.

Name: Bondsmen
Flavor Text: Every bond is tested. Steel holds longer than oaths.

Name: Huscarls
Flavor Text: The last to retreat. Often, the last to survive.

Name: Huntsmen
Flavor Text: They track prey through snow that swallows armies whole.

Name: Cavalry
Flavor Text: They ride to war like they ride to feast — loudly.

Name: Eye of the Raven
Original Text: The Norse player may re-roll a single D6 at any time during the rest of his turn, through the opponent's turn, or in his following turn (until end of next Command phase). Cannot be used on blunder Command rolls, but can re-roll the blunder chart result. Only one Eye of the Raven spell may be in effect per turn. The re-roll result cannot be changed or re-rolled again by any other means (Orb of Majesty, Banner of Fortune, etc.), and this spell cannot re-roll a die already re-rolled by another effect.
Current Text: Re-roll one single D6 at any point from now through the end of the opponent’s next turn (including their turn). Applies to any roll — orders, magic, artillery, shooting, or combat — but only ONE die in that roll (e.g. one die of a 3D6 drive back). Cannot be used for Instability or Giant Goes Wild. Only one Eye of the Raven active at a time.

Name: Bannermen
Original Text: Core infantry. Required for large brigades.
Current Text: Core infantry. Required for large brigades.
Flavor Text: Where the banner stands, the line holds.

Name: Crossbows
Original Text: Can be replaced by Handguns (same min/max count).
Current Text: Can be replaced by Handguns (same min/max count).
Flavor Text: The bolt cares nothing for the rank of its target.

Name: Handguns
Original Text: Armour piercing: -1 to enemy armour saves.
Current Text: Armour piercing: -1 to enemy armour saves.
Flavor Text: Thunder speaks where words have failed.

Name: Maneaters
Flavor Text: Far from home, and glad of it.

Name: Imperial Cavalry
Flavor Text: Jade armour. Iron discipline. Unstoppable momentum.

Name: Triple Bows
Flavor Text: Three bolts. One heartbeat. One fewer enemy.

Name: Ashigaru
Flavor Text: They hold the line so the samurai may advance.

Name: Ashigaru Bowmen
Flavor Text: Release together. Strike as one. Fall as none.
